# QuantaLLM

> On-device LLM inference for Android. Run large language models entirely on-device — no internet required.

## What is QuantaLLM?

QuantaLLM is an open-source Android application that enables fully private, offline LLM inference on consumer Android devices. It uses llama.cpp as its primary inference backend and ONNX Runtime as a secondary backend, with hardware acceleration via the Qualcomm Hexagon NPU (Neural Processing Unit).

All computation happens on-device. No data is transmitted, no telemetry is collected, and the app works in airplane mode.

## Key Capabilities

- **100% offline inference** — no cloud dependency
- **Hexagon NPU acceleration** — 2-5x speedup over CPU-only on Snapdragon devices
- **Dual inference backends** — llama.cpp (GGUF) and ONNX Runtime
- **AIDL cross-app API** — other Android apps can bind to QuantaLLM as a shared inference service
- **ARM64 optimized** — dotprod, i8mm, and bf16 native layer optimizations
- **90+ model architectures** — Llama 3, Mistral, Phi-3, Gemma 2, Qwen 2, and more
- **Streaming token generation** — real-time output with configurable sampling (temp, top-k, top-p, min-p)
- **No account required** — free, open source, Apache 2.0

## Hardware Requirements

- Android 12 (API 31) or later; Android 13+ recommended
- Minimum 6 GB RAM; 8 GB+ recommended
- Best performance on Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 or newer (Hexagon NPU required for NPU acceleration)
- Supported flagship devices: Samsung Galaxy S24 series, OnePlus 12, Xiaomi 14 Pro

## Supported Model Formats

- **GGUF** (via llama.cpp) — Q2 through Q8 quantization
- **ONNX** (via ONNX Runtime) — with QNN compilation for Hexagon acceleration

## Performance (Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, 7B Q4 model)

- Prompt processing (prefill): 100–300+ tokens/second
- Text generation: 15–30 tokens/second (CPU+NPU)
- With Hexagon HTP: up to 40+ tokens/second on smaller models

## Privacy

- Zero telemetry
- No prompt logging
- No network requests during inference
- Chat history stored in local encrypted SQLite database

## Links

- Homepage: https://quanta-web-pi.vercel.app
- Documentation: https://quanta-web-pi.vercel.app/docs

- License: Apache 2.0

## Current Version

v2.0.0 — Dual inference engine (llama.cpp + ONNX Runtime), Hexagon NPU acceleration, AIDL cross-app service, MVVM architecture with StateFlow.
